GHSA-P77W-8QQV-26RM.34816660251d704914204fcac35bdc08a8c70ba7:
chore(deps-dev): bump hono from 4.12.14 to 4.12.18 (#10875) Bumps [hono](https://github.com/honojs/hono) from 4.12.14 to 4.12.18.
Release notes

Sourced from hono's releases.

v4.12.18

Security fixes

This release includes fixes for the following security issues:

Cache Middleware ignores Vary: Authorization / Vary: Cookie leading to cross-user cache leakage

Affects: Cache Middleware. Fixes missing cache-skip handling for Vary: Authorization and Vary: Cookie, where a response cached for one authenticated user could be served to other users. GHSA-p77w-8qqv-26rm

CSS Declaration Injection via Style Object Values in JSX SSR

Affects: hono/jsx. Fixes a missing CSS-context escape for style object values and property names, where untrusted input could inject additional CSS declarations. The impact is limited to CSS and does not allow JavaScript execution. GHSA-qp7p-654g-cw7p

Improper validation of NumericDate claims (exp, nbf, iat) in JWT verify()

Affects: hono/utils/jwt. Fixes improper validation of exp, nbf, and iat claims, where falsy, non-finite, or non-numeric values could silently bypass time-based checks instead of being rejected per RFC 7519. GHSA-hm8q-7f3q-5f36

Users who use the JWT helper, hono/jsx, or the Cache middleware are strongly encouraged to upgrade to this version.

v4.12.17

What's Changed

  • fix(jsx): normalize SVG attributes on the root element by @​kfly8 in honojs/hono#4893
  • fix(ssg): add atom+xml and rss+xml to defaultExtensionMap by @​yuintei in honojs/hono#4899
  • fix(cors): make origin optional in CORSOptions by @​truffle-dev in honojs/hono#4905
  • fix(types): propagate middleware response types to app.on overloads by @​T4ko0522 in honojs/hono#4906

New Contributors

  • @​kfly8 made their first contribution in honojs/hono#4893
  • @​truffle-dev made their first contribution in honojs/hono#4905

Full Changelog: https://github.com/honojs/hono/compare/v4.12.16...v4.12.17

v4.12.16

Security fixes

This release includes fixes for the following security issues:

Unvalidated JSX Tag Names in hono/jsx May Allow HTML Injection

Affects: hono/jsx. Fixes missing validation of JSX tag names when using jsx() or createElement(), which could allow HTML injection if untrusted input is used as the tag name. GHSA-69xw-7hcm-h432

bodyLimit() can be bypassed for chunked / unknown-length requests

Affects: Body Limit Middleware. Fixes late enforcement for request bodies without a reliable Content-Length (e.g. chunked requests), where oversized requests could reach handlers and return successful responses before being rejected. GHSA-9vqf-7f2p-gf9v

chore(deps-dev): bump hono from 4.12.14 to 4.12.18 (#10875) Bumps [hono](https://github.com/honojs/hono) from 4.12.14 to 4.12.18.
Release notes

Sourced from hono's releases.

v4.12.18

Security fixes

This release includes fixes for the following security issues:

Cache Middleware ignores Vary: Authorization / Vary: Cookie leading to cross-user cache leakage

Affects: Cache Middleware. Fixes missing cache-skip handling for Vary: Authorization and Vary: Cookie, where a response cached for one authenticated user could be served to other users. GHSA-p77w-8qqv-26rm

CSS Declaration Injection via Style Object Values in JSX SSR

Affects: hono/jsx. Fixes a missing CSS-context escape for style object values and property names, where untrusted input could inject additional CSS declarations. The impact is limited to CSS and does not allow JavaScript execution. GHSA-qp7p-654g-cw7p

Improper validation of NumericDate claims (exp, nbf, iat) in JWT verify()

Affects: hono/utils/jwt. Fixes improper validation of exp, nbf, and iat claims, where falsy, non-finite, or non-numeric values could silently bypass time-based checks instead of being rejected per RFC 7519. GHSA-hm8q-7f3q-5f36

Users who use the JWT helper, hono/jsx, or the Cache middleware are strongly encouraged to upgrade to this version.

v4.12.17

What's Changed

  • fix(jsx): normalize SVG attributes on the root element by @​kfly8 in honojs/hono#4893
  • fix(ssg): add atom+xml and rss+xml to defaultExtensionMap by @​yuintei in honojs/hono#4899
  • fix(cors): make origin optional in CORSOptions by @​truffle-dev in honojs/hono#4905
  • fix(types): propagate middleware response types to app.on overloads by @​T4ko0522 in honojs/hono#4906

New Contributors

  • @​kfly8 made their first contribution in honojs/hono#4893
  • @​truffle-dev made their first contribution in honojs/hono#4905

Full Changelog: https://github.com/honojs/hono/compare/v4.12.16...v4.12.17

v4.12.16

Security fixes

This release includes fixes for the following security issues:

Unvalidated JSX Tag Names in hono/jsx May Allow HTML Injection

Affects: hono/jsx. Fixes missing validation of JSX tag names when using jsx() or createElement(), which could allow HTML injection if untrusted input is used as the tag name. GHSA-69xw-7hcm-h432

bodyLimit() can be bypassed for chunked / unknown-length requests

Affects: Body Limit Middleware. Fixes late enforcement for request bodies without a reliable Content-Length (e.g. chunked requests), where oversized requests could reach handlers and return successful responses before being rejected. GHSA-9vqf-7f2p-gf9v

v4.12.15

What's Changed

  • fix(jwt): support single-line PEM keys by @​hiendv in honojs/hono#4889

CVE-2026-41242.345fd642fb47373cc82e22efc37cdc3da4683d52:
fix(deps): patch 7 critical/high Dependabot security alerts (#10734)

## Security Alert Patch

Resolves 7 Dependabot security alerts in the critical + high severity
tier by bumping 4 transitive dependencies via root `pnpm.overrides`.

### Packages Updated

| Package | Old Constraint | New Constraint | Resolved | Strategy |
Scope | CVEs / GHSAs |

|---------|---------------|----------------|----------|----------|-------|--------------|
| `protobufjs` | `^7.2.5` | `^7.5.5` | 7.5.4 → 7.5.5 | C (override) |
dev-only* | CVE-2026-41242 (critical) |
| `basic-ftp` | `>=5.2.0` | `>=5.3.0` | 5.2.0 → 5.3.0 | C (override) |
dev-only* | GHSA-rp42-5vxx-qpwr, GHSA-6v7q-wjvx-w8wg, CVE-2026-39983 (3×
high) |
| `vite` | *(new)* | `^7.3.2` | 7.3.1 → 7.3.2 | C (override) | dev-only
| CVE-2026-39363, CVE-2026-39364 (2× high) |
| `defu` | *(new)* | `^6.1.5` | 6.1.4 → 6.1.7 | C (override) | dev-only
| CVE-2026-35209 (high) |

Strategy C (`pnpm.overrides`) is used because all vulnerable packages
are transitive dependencies already managed by the existing overrides
mechanism in this repo.

\* Reachable only through the `examples` workspace (marked `private:
true`) via dev/release tooling (release-it, vitest, tsdown, grpc). No
published `@langchain/*` package pulls these through a production path —
verified via `pnpm why -r --prod`.

### Minimum Safe Version

All bumps use bounded caret ranges (`^`) to stay within the current
major line:
- `^7.5.5` keeps protobufjs in the 7.x line (there is a 8.0.x available)
- `^7.3.2` keeps vite in the 7.x line (there is a 8.0.x available)
- `^6.1.5` keeps defu in the 6.x line
- `>=5.3.0` keeps basic-ftp at minor bump only (no 6.x exists)

No major-version crossings. All engines/type/module-format fields are
unchanged between the old and new versions.

### CVE Details

- **CVE-2026-41242** / GHSA-xq3m-2v4x-88gg — Arbitrary code execution in
protobufjs
- **GHSA-rp42-5vxx-qpwr** — basic-ftp DoS via unbounded memory in
`Client.list()`
- **GHSA-6v7q-wjvx-w8wg** — basic-ftp incomplete CRLF injection
protection in MKD
- **CVE-2026-39983** / GHSA-chqc-8p9q-pq6q — basic-ftp FTP command
injection via CRLF
- **CVE-2026-39363** / GHSA-p9ff-h696-f583 — Vite arbitrary file read
via dev server WebSocket
- **CVE-2026-39364** / GHSA-v2wj-q39q-566r — Vite `server.fs.deny`
bypassed with queries
- **CVE-2026-35209** / GHSA-737v-mqg7-c878 — defu prototype pollution
via `__proto__`

### Deferred (medium tier)

Per the patch workflow, only the highest-severity open tier is batched.
The following 9 medium-severity alerts remain open and will be patched
in a follow-up:

- hono (5 alerts)
- @hono/node-server (1 alert)
- follow-redirects (1 alert)
- vite (1 medium, covered incidentally by this bump)
- hono write path cookie validation (1 alert)

### Verification

- [x] `pnpm install` succeeds with the new lockfile (44 packages
fetched; Socket Firewall pass)
- [x] `oxlint .` — 0 warnings / 0 errors across 1888 files
- [x] `@langchain/core` test suite — 1329 passed / 1 skipped (smoke test
confirming vite 7.3.2 + vitest)
- [x] `@langchain/core` build — successful via tsdown (smoke test
confirming defu)
- [x] `gitleaks git --staged --verbose` — no leaks
- [x] CI: full test matrix + type check

### Linear Tickets

Linear ticket lookup skipped — CLI not authenticated in this
environment. No ticket IDs in title; if any Linear issues track these
CVEs, please close them manually on merge.

CVE-2024-29415.389e0f7b5f30948d34051e7aad349f3743381066:
Fix CVE-2024-29415: upgrade azion to v3 to remove vulnerable ip package

The `ip` package (<= 2.0.1) has a high severity SSRF vulnerability.
Upgrading `azion` from ^1.11.1 to ^3.0.0 eliminates the transitive
dependency on `ip`, which was replaced with `ip-cidr` in azion v3.

pnpm override forces all transitive `tar` dependencies to resolve to
the patched version (7.5.4+).
